rules.description: Opening play prefers central pawn control, fast minor-piece development, king safety, and avoiding repeated piece moves before development is complete.
Increase move scores that preserve castling rights, complete kingside development, and reduce early queen exposure unless tactics are concrete. Castling is a strategic move, not only a king move.

Maintain a protection network: valuable pieces should be defended by friendly pieces, hanging pieces are urgent liabilities, and unfavorable trades against cheaper attackers must be avoided unless compensation is found.
Use local PGN and engine analysis to learn named opening families, common replies, and solution branches; do not guess a fixed answer when the opening book lacks samples.
Middlegame search emphasizes king pressure, forcing moves, piece activity, pawn breaks, and conversion of static advantages into active threats.
Endgame play values active king position, passed pawn creation, opposition-like distance features, rook activity, and exact tablebase results when available.

decisionStack: endgame: Syzygy if LOCAL_SYZYGY_PATH exists and piece count <= 7; else Stockfish/local search
